title: Uruguayan Bamboo Shoot Casserole
description: A hearty, intensely flavored casserole featuring bamboo shoots and shio-cure, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This dish is Jain-friendly and can be made ahead for meal prep.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the bamboo shoots, shio-cure, salt, basil, and thyme to the skillet. Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the bamboo shoots start to soften.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and vegetable broth until smooth.
Add the butter to the bowl and stir until melted.
Pour the wet ingredients over the bamboo shoot mixture and stir until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a cedar plank or a baking dish.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
